blocks.content: That afternoon I went back to the notebook. Sat at the desk.
I wrote what I'd figured out over the last few weeks. Not the events — the actual things.
'You already know what to do. The question is whether you trust it when your heart's going.' — Mr. Cole's car.
'The good decision and the scary decision are usually the same one.'
'The messy version is closer to true.'
I sat there looking at what I'd written. It looked like someone who was figuring something out.
I thought about next week. What Daniel being quiet meant. What Crystal's 'good night' meant. What Stacy in the bleachers meant for Jonathan.
I didn't have answers for any of it. But I had the notebook. And I had something I hadn't had before any of this started — I knew how to sit with not knowing.
The light was going. The block was settling into Saturday night. I put the pen down.
Whatever came next was already on its way.
